overview:
The AWS Certified Generative AI Developer - Professional exam validates a candidate's ability to design, implement, and deploy generative AI solutions using AWS services. It focuses on the practical application of Large Language Models (LLMs), prompt engineering, RAG architectures, and the integration of foundation models into production-grade applications.

This exam is designed for developers and AI engineers who have experience building generative AI applications. Candidates should be proficient in Python, familiar with the AWS SDK, and have a deep understanding of how to orchestrate AI workflows while maintaining security and cost-efficiency.
recertification_info: Recertify by retaking the current version of the exam or passing a newer version of the certification.

official_resources.title: AWS Skill Builder - Generative AI Learning Path

preparation_tips: Master the differences between various Foundation Models (FMs) available in Amazon Bedrock to choose the right model for specific use cases.
Deep dive into Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns, specifically focusing on knowledge bases and vector databases.
Practice advanced prompt engineering techniques including few-shot, chain-of-thought, and system prompting.
Understand the nuances of model fine-tuning versus RAG and when to apply each strategy.
Study the implementation of Guardrails for Amazon Bedrock to ensure safety and compliance.
Learn how to optimize LLM performance using caching strategies and token management to reduce costs.
Get hands-on experience with LangChain or LlamaIndex for orchestrating complex AI workflows on AWS.
Study the shared responsibility model specifically as it applies to Generative AI and data privacy.
Review AWS IAM policies for granting granular access to Bedrock models and data sources.
Practice troubleshooting common GenAI issues such as hallucinations and latency bottlenecks.
exam_day_tips: Carefully read the scenario to determine if the requirement is for 'lowest cost', 'lowest latency', or 'highest accuracy'.
Use the process of elimination for options that suggest non-AWS native tools unless specified in the prompt.
Manage your time strictly; if a complex architecture question takes too long, flag it and move on.
Pay close attention to keywords like 'least operational overhead' when choosing between managed and custom solutions.
Ensure you distinguish between 'fine-tuning' and 'continued pre-training' in the answer choices.
Double-check that the selected solution meets all the compliance and safety requirements mentioned in the prompt.
recommended_experience: 2+ years of software development experience and 1+ year of hands-on experience building GenAI applications on AWS.
